gate.help: Don't have your code? Reach us on WhatsApp.

greeting.title: Welcome
greeting.p1: Dear family, with so much joy we share with you everything you'll need to be with us on one of the most important days of our lives.
greeting.p2: We know what it means to cross continents, request days off, and move heaven and earth to make it to Trieste. Every one of you is making an enormous effort — and we want you to know: we see it, we treasure it, and it moves us deeply.
greeting.p3: The only thing we truly need on that day is to have you near. Your presence, your laughter, the stories shared around the table — that's the most beautiful gift we could ever ask for.
greeting.p4: And to our parents, in particular — endless thanks for your unconditional support, every single day and at every step of the way.
greeting.p5: First of all, a thousand thanks to those of you who have already confirmed. To those who haven't yet, please let us know as soon as you can so we can organise every detail with care.
greeting.p6: Thank you for being there — before, during, and after. We'll be waiting for you in Italy with our hearts wide open.

travel.title: The journey
travel.p1: We recommend flying into **Venice Marco Polo Airport**. From Venice to Trieste it's roughly **2 hours by car**.
travel.p2: Our suggestion is to spend **1 or 2 nights in Venice** before continuing on to Trieste. October is low season, so you'll get the city at its best — and arrive rested for the wedding.

hotelVenice.title: Recommended hotel in Venice
hotelVenice.name: Hampton by Hilton Venice Isola Nuova
hotelVenice.note: A 2-minute walk from the water taxi that takes you to the historic centre.

transport.title: Transport
transport.sub: the wedding day & getting from venice
transport.p1: For the **wedding day** we've arranged **round-trip transport** between the hotel and San Giusto Cathedral, so you don't have to worry about anything that afternoon.
transport.p2intro: **By public transport from Venice**, the trip is roughly 3 hours total and happens in three legs:
steps: **Bus** from Marco Polo Airport to **Venezia Mestre station** (~25 minutes, ~€10 per person).
**Train** from Mestre to **Trieste Centrale** (~2 hours, frequent departures, **€15–30 per person**).
**Taxi** from Trieste Centrale to the resort (~20 minutes, **€40–60**).
transport.mapsLabel: See the full route on Google Maps
transport.byCar: **Or, you can rent a car** at Venice Airport — about 2 hours direct to Portopiccolo. **The resort includes parking**, and having a car gives you freedom to explore the area.
transport.callout: Yes — we know getting to us is a small adventure on its own. *Thank you in advance for making the trip.* Any questions about transfers, just write to us anytime.

lodging.title: Where to stay
lodging.p1: We've reserved the **whole hotel** so every room is available for the family. The reception and party are *at the same resort* — by far the most comfortable option for everyone.

resort.title: Tivoli Portopiccolo Sistiana Wellness Resort & Spa

lodging.p2: We recommend staying **at least 2 nights** — the 18th and 19th of October — so you can enjoy the wedding day and the day after without any rush.
lodging.callout: Please let us know **how many rooms** you need, and **how many nights with which exact dates**. With that information we'll confirm your booking with the hotel.

bigDay.title: The big day

schedule.event: Transport leaves the hotel for the church
Transport leaves the church for the resort
Reception and cocktail at Tivoli Portopiccolo

bigDay.note: * Reception times are approximate — the idea is to enjoy without rushing.

dayBefore.title: The day before
dayBefore.p1: In the afternoon we want to **eat pizza in central Trieste**, or maybe **head to the seaside for drinks and fresh fish** — whatever feels right that day. The plan is to be spontaneous and flexible — anyone who wants to join us is more than welcome!

rsvp.title: RSVP
rsvp.sub: we'd love to hear from you
rsvp.intro: You can confirm by WhatsApp or call at either of these numbers:
rsvp.label: Confirm attendance

places.title: Things to do
places.aboutTrieste: Trieste is a city of borders: at once Italian, Austro-Hungarian and Slovenian. Perched on the Adriatic and ringed by limestone hills, it has neoclassical palaces, literary cafés, and sunsets that stay with you. It's a city to walk slowly, with an Aperol in hand.
places.intro: If you have extra time before or after the wedding, here are a few of our favourite places — all worth a visit.

miramare.name: Miramare Castle
miramare.p1: The most romantic white castle in Italy, built by Archduke Maximilian of Habsburg in the 19th century, perched right above the Adriatic Sea.
miramare.p2: The gardens are huge and free to enter — perfect for a slow walk. Just 15 minutes from the hotel.

trieste.name: Piazza Unità d'Italia
trieste.p1: The largest sea-facing square in Europe, framed by 18th- and 19th-century palaces and dominated by the majestic City Hall.
trieste.p2: Sunset here, an Aperol in hand, is one of those moments you don't forget.

sangiusto.name: San Giusto Castle
sangiusto.p1: Right next to the cathedral where we're getting married. Climb up to the walls for the best view of the whole city and the gulf.

sistiana.name: Sistiana Bay
sistiana.p1: The hotel sits literally on this bay. The Portopiccolo seafront is gorgeous at dusk — there are fresh-fish restaurants and bars right by the water.

venice.name: Venice
venice.p1: Two hours by car or train. In October, free of summer crowds, you get the very best of the city. We recommend a night or two before or after the wedding.

canal.name: Canal Grande
canal.p1: An 18th-century canal lined with neoclassical palaces, boats moored along the quay, and the church of Sant'Antonio Nuovo closing the perspective at the far end.
canal.p2: At sunset the golden light reflects on the water — one of the most photogenic corners of Trieste.

molo.name: Molo Audace
molo.p1: An 18th-century stone pier reaching out into the Adriatic. Walking to the very end at sunset, the city behind you and the open sea ahead, is an absolute must-do.

faro.name: Faro della Vittoria
faro.p1: A 70-metre lighthouse-monument completed in 1927, dedicated to fallen sailors. Climb to the top for a 360° view over the city and the gulf.

caffe.name: Historic cafés
caffe.p1: Trieste is Italy's coffee capital (home to Illy and Hausbrandt). Sitting at Caffè degli Specchi on Piazza Unità, or at Caffè San Marco — where Joyce and Svevo wrote — is an essential ritual.

grotta.name: Grotta Gigante
grotta.p1: One of the largest tourist caves in the world, 20 minutes from Trieste. If you love an underground adventure, it's worth the detour.
